content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of length NNN, we obtain its score as follows.

Perform the following operation N‚àí2N-2N‚àí2 times:


If gcd‚Å°(A1,A3)‚â§gcd‚Å°(A2,A3)\gcd(A_1, A_3) \leq \gcd(A_2, A_3)gcd(A1‚Äã,A3‚Äã)‚â§gcd(A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã), delete A1A_1A1‚Äã from AAA. Otherwise, delete A2A_2A2‚Äã.

After the deletion, the remaining elements are re-indexed to start from 111.
In the end, the array will have exactly two elements remaining.

Its score is defined to be the first of these two elements.

For example, consider A=[4,3,2,1]A = [4, 3, 2, 1]A=[4,3,2,1]. The process is as follows:


First, gcd‚Å°(A1,A3)=2\gcd(A_1, A_3) = 2gcd(A1‚Äã,A3‚Äã)=2 and gcd‚Å°(A2,A3)=1\gcd(A_2, A_3) = 1gcd(A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã)=1.

Since gcd‚Å°(A1,A3)>gcd‚Å°(A2,A3)\gcd(A_1, A_3) \gt \gcd(A_2, A_3)gcd(A1‚Äã,A3‚Äã)>gcd(A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã), we delete element A2A_2A2‚Äã from AAA.

The array is now A=[4,2,1]A = [4, 2, 1]A=[4,2,1].
Next, gcd‚Å°(A1,A3)=gcd‚Å°(A2,A3)=1\gcd(A_1, A_3) = \gcd(A_2, A_3) = 1gcd(A1‚Äã,A3‚Äã)=gcd(A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã)=1.

Since gcd‚Å°(A1,A3)‚â§gcd‚Å°(A2,A3)\gcd(A_1, A_3) \leq \gcd(A_2, A_3)gcd(A1‚Äã,A3‚Äã)‚â§gcd(A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã), we delete element A1A_1A1‚Äã from AAA.

The array is now A=[2,1]A = [2, 1]A=[2,1].
The first remaining element is 222, so that's the score of the array.

You are given three integers N,X,N, X,N,X, and YYY. Find **any** array AAA satisfying the following three conditions:


AAA has length NNN, and consists of **distinct** integers between 111 and NNN.
AN=XA_N = XAN‚Äã=X
AN‚àí1=YA_{N-1} = YAN‚àí1‚Äã=Y
score(A)‚âÝY\text{score}(A) \neq Yscore(A)ÓÄÝ=Y
If no such array AAA exists, output ‚àí1-1‚àí1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing three space-separated integers N,X,N,X,N,X, and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case:



If no valid array AAA exists, output the single integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1 on a new line.
Otherwise, output NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, denoting the array you found.

Note that the AiA_iAi‚Äã should be distinct integers in [1,N][1, N][1,N].

If there are multiple valid arrays, any of them will be accepted.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: No valid array exists.
Test case 222: Consider A=[3,1,2,4,5,6]A = [3, 1, 2, 4, 5, 6]A=[3,1,2,4,5,6]. The process is as follows:

gcd‚Å°(3,2)=1‚â§gcd‚Å°(1,2)=1\gcd(3, 2) = 1 \leq \gcd(1, 2) = 1gcd(3,2)=1‚â§gcd(1,2)=1. So, 333 gets deleted.
The array is [1,2,4,5,6][1, 2, 4, 5, 6][1,2,4,5,6].
gcd‚Å°(1,4)=1\gcd(1, 4) = 1gcd(1,4)=1 and gcd‚Å°(2,4)=2\gcd(2, 4) = 2gcd(2,4)=2. So, 111 gets deleted.
The array is [2,4,5,6][2, 4, 5, 6][2,4,5,6].
gcd‚Å°(2,5)=1\gcd(2, 5) = 1gcd(2,5)=1 and gcd‚Å°(4,5)=1\gcd(4, 5) = 1gcd(4,5)=1. So, 222 gets deleted.
The array is [4,5,6][4, 5, 6][4,5,6].
gcd‚Å°(4,6)=2\gcd(4, 6) = 2gcd(4,6)=2 and gcd‚Å°(5,6)=1\gcd(5, 6) = 1gcd(5,6)=1. So, 555 gets deleted.
The array is [4,6][4, 6][4,6].
The process ends. The leftmost remaining element is 444, which is not equal to Y=5Y = 5Y=5 as we wanted.

There are other valid arrays, any of them will be accepted.
